2Red text \- features we do not have

These are the roles that use the product regularly / frequently. 

1. We are building this granular view of their day in life on trmeric to drive up the value they get from the platform, and in the process get higher usage rates  
2. We want to make sure that the first experience on trmeric is great  
3. Should we move to using the new Tango (where work comes to me)?

**I am a project manager, new to trmeric.**

The way I operate is with standard project management systems. Setting up projects, updating them, closing them out. There always is a bunch of reporting I need to do.

I am told to use trmeric 

* I signed in using a single sign on (do I?)  
* Or I had to sign in first, the link had come in an email  
  * It was a well drafted email, with clear instructions on what to do  
  * I log in, they ask me to change my password  
* Then I land on trmeric  
  * It is a clean conversational interface, but I have some chips that suggest what I could do  
  * A pop up appears, and ‘Tango’ introduces itself as my chief of staff  
  * It also asks if I’d like a tour which I agree to  
  * The tour guide walks be through my options  
    * It seems there are bunch of different options  
  * Tango suggests I see my set of projects which have already been uploaded for me  
* I get to a new page (the Workspace)  
  * It's a classy, simple interface  
  * I see some graphs on top with insights  
  * And I see some projects listed on the page as well *(assumption \- this happens only if the projects are pre-loaded)*  
  * Tango is here too\! It says would I like a tour, of-course I do  
* I see some *interesting insights* right upfront  
  * I see the projects that need attention  
  * I see the milestones that need my attention (this is important if the PMs have limited projects)  
  * Tango says there are more insights \-   
    * I see some Tango suggestions on the projects I am running, that's interesting, how did it think about it?  
    * I also get to see the impact of projects I am working on. Now how did they get that I wonder\!\!  
* The space below has a listing of my projects  
  * Tango helps me understand the columns better, the good news is that they are configurable   
  * The double-click of each project takes me to the milestone information / status  
  * The Left hand side is interesting as well  
    * We need to think what to do here because the span of control is very limited with 2-3 projects   
    * 

**I am a Demand Manager**

1. I receive a link to trmeric and proceed to click that  
   * I have SSO so I get onto my interface   
2. I see a dialogue box with a set of options below  
3. An agent also introduces itself saying that is my chief of staff and that it can give me a tour  
   * I am introduced to a bunch of capabilities  
   * I even have a voice over for this\!  
4. I click on one of the chips / modes and Tango has an illustrative response for me  
5. I see links in the answers that take me to that particular mission  
6. Tango then recommends that I get onto what looks like a dashboard  
7. Here too Tango pops and says he can give me a tour (this tour guide tango needs to pop, it's not fun right now)  
   * It also says why these features / capabilities are there  
   * I see some analytics / trends on the top and a list of demands below that  
     * Need to check as to whether trmeric comes loaded with existed tech requests or it does not  
     * Need to check as to how many missions does a demand manager handle on an average  
   * The analytics are interesting  
     * I can see what is the nature of work / requests I am working on  
     * I can see how much time it is taking to move missions from intake to execution, and even what takes a most time  
     * I also get to see how my work contributes to corporate / business priorities   
   * Below the analytics, I can either in review mode or action mode (but it doubles up like an action)  
     * The review mode has all the missions  
   * On the LHS nav bar  
     * I see the actions I should be taking  
     * I also see the actions that Tango as taken for me  
     * Can we show the count of actions we have taken (43)? That way the users understand what all got done  
8. Action-shots  
   * Each card summarizes the state of the mission, and the action I should take, if any. This is great because it brings together the complete picture for me and I do not have to go around pulling it from different places  
   * Best to enhance actions further make them more effective, action oriented (like the one in the snapshot below)  
   * If I can get very effective instructions about what to do, and if it can also do them for me, it would be great (for example building out user stories with some background on what needs to be done)  
   * It also gives me a sense of how I should organize my week, the must do, the nice to do, stuff that I can push out. Along with the rationale behind it

   ![][image1]

9. What if all this was possible with Tango? Do we need to build all this UI?  
   * Would Tango be able to customize the recommendations by role? As an example would it have a different recommendation for Portfolio managers as compared to project managers? And would it do it consistently?  
10. How would this be useful for a midsized enterprise where there is no DM role, the same person is performing both the DM and the PM (project) role?  
    *   
      *   
11. Tango asks me to click one of the missions  
    * What opens up is a cool looking UI which summarizes that mission  
    * There are tabs for other aspects of the mission as well  
    * Here too Tango pops up and suggests that he can help me with the tour  
    * There always is Tango, so I know who to go to for help  
12. Then I get to creating a new mission  
    * This would 

Build a better understanding:

* Look at a few project manager views (where there are only 2-3 projects)  
* Look at a few projects  
* Day in life interviews

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
